humanSummary.text: Initial audits found P1 screenshot staging and process cleanup gaps. The branch repaired managed staging, non-zero cleanup, and AfterExit hook cleanup bypasses, and final re-audit found no remaining P0/P1 blockers. Host-availability and additional hardening items are recorded as follow-ups.

findings.title: Browser host availability capability field
findings.description: Browser capabilities are advertised structurally even when no browser host is present. Host unavailability is communicated through limitations and runtime failure, but automation would benefit from a machine-readable host availability field.

evidence.detail: Final Utsushi audit found no P0/P1 blockers and recommended a machine-readable host availability field for browser adapters.

findings.impact: Automated planning may need to attempt a run to distinguish unsupported host state from supported capture capability.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Browser adapter capability output includes machine-readable host availability or equivalent diagnostic state.
CLI capability tests cover host-present and host-missing behavior where practical.

proposedDagNode.title: Browser host availability diagnostics
proposedDagNode.summary: Expose machine-readable browser host availability diagnostics for Utsushi browser launch adapters.
deliverables: Browser adapter capability output with host availability or equivalent diagnostic state
CLI capability tests for browser host missing and host configured paths
acceptanceCriteria: Automation can distinguish missing browser host from supported browser capture capability without launching a smoke run.
Host availability diagnostics do not expose raw private local paths.
auditFocus: Browser adapter capabilities overclaiming host readiness

findings.title: Successful-exit AfterExit cleanup coverage
findings.description: The repaired P1 covers non-zero exit plus failing AfterExit hook. If the desired contract is that all AfterExit hook failures clean up, a successful-exit plus failing hook branch should get explicit coverage.

evidence.detail: Final Utsushi audit found the prior non-zero combo fixed and suggested successful-exit AfterExit coverage if that broader contract is desired.

findings.impact: The intended cleanup behavior for successful child exit plus AfterExit hook failure is less explicit than the non-zero failure path.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: The AfterExit hook failure cleanup policy for successful child exits is documented.
A focused test covers successful child exit plus AfterExit hook failure if cleanup is required.

proposedDagNode.title: Successful-exit AfterExit cleanup coverage
proposedDagNode.summary: Clarify and test cleanup behavior for successful child exits followed by failing AfterExit hooks.
deliverables: Documented AfterExit hook failure cleanup policy for successful child exits
Focused utsushi-core regression test when cleanup is required
acceptanceCriteria: The successful-exit AfterExit failure policy is explicit in code comments or docs.
The selected policy is covered by a focused test.
auditFocus: Successful exit plus AfterExit hook failure cleanup ambiguity

findings.title: Browser staging path stale-data guard
findings.description: Browser staging is now under the managed root, but clearing or exclusively creating the staging screenshot path before launch would reduce stale-data risk after interrupted runs.

evidence.detail: Final Utsushi audit accepted managed staging and suggested a stale-data guard for interrupted prior runs.

findings.impact: Interrupted runs could leave stale staging data under the managed root that a later run might encounter.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Browser screenshot staging path is exclusively created or cleared before launch.
Tests cover stale staging screenshot data from an interrupted prior run.

proposedDagNode.title: Browser staging stale-data guard
proposedDagNode.summary: Prevent stale screenshot staging data from interrupted browser adapter runs from affecting later captures.
deliverables: Exclusive create or pre-launch cleanup for browser screenshot staging paths
Regression test with stale staging data under the managed artifact root
acceptanceCriteria: Stale browser staging data cannot be read as a fresh screenshot artifact.
The stale-data guard keeps all temporary screenshot bytes inside the managed artifact root.
auditFocus: Stale browser screenshot staging data after interrupted runs
